title: Are Online Classes Really Worth It?
subtitle: 在线课程真的物有所值吗

paragraphs.en: Online classes began to be popularized just a few decades ago. They are advertised as a way for adults to finish their education and students to learn the material at their own pace — it is far more compatible for people with busy schedules. But after being enrolled in an online course last fall semester, I came to realize online classes were merely a means to fulfil course requirements.
paragraphs.zh: 在线课程仅在几十年前才开始普及，被宣传为成人完成学业、学生自主学习的一种方式——对日程繁忙的人更为友好。但去年秋季学期选了一门在线课程后，我意识到在线课程不过是完成学分要求的工具。

paragraphs.en: First of all, students lack the desire to learn, and they simply complete their assignments to receive credit for a passing grade rather than genuinely engage with the course material. As online courses tend to have more than 100 students, most of the assignments are short and simple. They are not designed for students to interact with the material in depth but designed to be graded easily to accommodate such a large number of students.
paragraphs.zh: 首先，学生缺乏学习动力，只是完成作业换取及格学分，而非真正深入课程内容。由于在线课程通常有超过100名学生，大多数作业都很短且简单。这些作业不是为了让学生深入探讨内容，而是为了方便批改大量学生的作业。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps the biggest disadvantage of taking an online class is the absence of face-to-face interaction between the teacher and their students. Live sessions are infrequent and are often scheduled during the middle of the day when students have to attend other classes or work. The office hours of the professor may also be during inconvenient times for many students as well. Most interaction with the professor has to be through email which is often impersonal. It is nearly impossible for students to build a relationship with their professor. There is also little interaction amongst students. It can be harder for students to create study groups and form relationships with their peers.
paragraphs.zh: 也许在线课程最大的缺点是师生之间缺乏面对面互动。实时课堂很少，常安排在白天——而学生那时往往要上其他课或工作。教授的答疑时间对很多学生来说也不方便。与教授的大部分交流只能通过邮件，往往显得冷淡而缺乏个人化。学生几乎不可能与教授建立关系，学生之间的互动也很少。组建学习小组、与同学建立友谊也更加困难。

words.meaning: adj. 非个人化的；冷淡的

paragraphs.en: Online classes also require either a computer or laptop and a reliable internet connection. Not all students have access to these types of resources, whether it is for financial or other reasons, and some students can be put at a disadvantage. Offering online classes certainly helps students who would otherwise not be able to attend classroom sessions. However, they fail to provide a genuine education with an emphasis on convenience rather than critical thinking. We need restructured online classes in which students can have a learning experience that will actually provide quality education.
paragraphs.zh: 在线课程还需要电脑和稳定的网络连接。并非所有学生都能获得这些资源，无论是经济原因还是其他原因，部分学生因此处于劣势。提供在线课程确实帮助了那些无法到教室上课的学生，但它们未能提供真正的教育——重便利而轻批判性思维。我们需要重新设计在线课程，让学生真正获得高质量的学习体验。
